purpose: Claim-level source, limitation, and visual-evidence map for the ORVYQ evidence-led rebuild.

status_policy.verified: The claim is supportable from the listed primary or authoritative source.
status_policy.attributed_commentary: The statement may remain only when clearly attributed on screen and in narration.
status_policy.rewrite_required: The current script wording is not sufficiently sourced or is too specific; full render is blocked until rewritten.
status_policy.source_required: A primary or authoritative source and visual evidence asset must be added before use.

source_catalog.title: Agentic Misalignment: How LLMs could be insider threats
source_catalog.usage_note: Any recreated visual must be labelled 'ORVYQ recreation from Anthropic Research' and retain the controlled-simulation limitation.

source_catalog.title: Announcing our updated Responsible Scaling Policy
Detecting and countering misuse of AI: August 2025
source_catalog.limitation: Company disclosure about misuse of its own system; not independently verified in every detail.

source_catalog.title: Disrupting the first reported AI-orchestrated cyber espionage campaign
source_catalog.limitation: Provider-authored investigation; wording must preserve 'reported' and attribution.

source_catalog.title: Activating AI Safety Level 3 protections
source_catalog.limitation: Anthropic said it had not definitively determined the model crossed the threshold; measures were precautionary and provisional.

source_catalog.title: Generative AI and Jobs: A Refined Global Index of Occupational Exposure
source_catalog.limitation: Exposure is not a forecast of job loss; transformation is assessed as more likely than wholesale replacement.

source_catalog.title: Workers’ exposure to AI: What indicators tell us – and what they don’t
source_catalog.limitation: Exposure measures should not be treated as direct unemployment forecasts.

source_catalog.title: General-purpose AI obligations under the AI Act
source_catalog.limitation: The threshold can be updated and designation is not determined by compute alone.

source_catalog.title: Guidelines on obligations for General-Purpose AI providers

source_catalog.publisher: US National Telecommunications and Information Administration
source_catalog.title: Dual-Use Foundation Models with Widely Available Model Weights Report
source_catalog.limitation: The report emphasizes uncertainty and marginal risk relative to closed models and other technologies.

source_catalog.title: AI Safety Institute approach to evaluations
source_catalog.limitation: Evaluations are preliminary, not comprehensive declarations that a system is safe.

sections.title: What the safety tests actually show
sections.dramatic_function: Move from abstract fear to a concrete controlled experiment, then state limits.

sections.title: Why slowing down alone is not a stable choice
Cyber and biological capability pressure
sections.dramatic_function: Separate real reported misuse from capability evaluations.

sections.title: Forecast uncertainty and durable concentration
sections.dramatic_function: Contrast forecasts, then shift from employment speculation to power concentration.

sections.title: Control can also concentrate power
sections.dramatic_function: Show that regulation is necessary but distributional consequences matter.

sections.title: Open capability versus closed control
sections.dramatic_function: Present a genuine trade-off without declaring a simple winner.

sections.title: What useful safety would actually require
sections.dramatic_function: Turn diagnosis into technical, procedural and structural safeguards.

sections.title: The danger may be the system of incentives
sections.dramatic_function: Return to the opening question and resolve on human agency.

claims.narration_excerpt: Every major AI lab on Earth believes moving this fast might be dangerous.
evidence_requirements: Show at least two real framework titles with publisher and date.
Do not imply all labs hold identical views.

claims.narration_excerpt: If they slow down, somebody else won't.
evidence_requirements: Frame as the film's incentive analysis, not a measured universal belief.

claims.narration_excerpt: Capability moves faster than our ability to understand it, control it, or govern it.
evidence_requirements: Use timeline or process contrast, not a fabricated speed chart.

claims.narration_excerpt: Researchers deliberately pushed frontier models toward their limits.
evidence_requirements: Show official article title/date.
Show that 16 models were tested.
State controlled simulations.

claims.narration_excerpt: A model given access to fictional emails threatened to expose a fabricated affair.
evidence_requirements: Use a labelled recreation of the fictional email interface or the official figure.
Explicitly state fictional company, fictional people and simulated scenario.

claims.narration_excerpt: None of this happened in the wild.
evidence_requirements: Display the source's controlled-simulation limitation at main-text size.
Do not bury this limitation as a small footnote.

claims.narration_excerpt: Markets reward whoever ships the most capable model first.
evidence_requirements: Add authoritative market or company evidence; do not use decorative stock tickers.

claims.narration_excerpt: A rival's model reportedly overtook one leading company's flagship ... internal emergency.
evidence_requirements: Replace with a fully attributable, documented competitive event or remove the sentence.
claims.recommended_rewrite: Recent model releases have repeatedly compressed the time between a rival's breakthrough and a competitor's response.

claims.narration_excerpt: A criminal used a coding model to automate reconnaissance, steal credentials, and extort organizations.
evidence_requirements: Show the report title/date and case summary.
Retain company-attribution limitation.

claims.narration_excerpt: An AI agent carried out the large majority of the technical intrusion work.
evidence_requirements: Use 'reported by Anthropic'.
Show 80–90% figure only with source/date and provider-authored limitation.

claims.narration_excerpt: Some models now give a meaningful capability boost ... enough to trigger additional safeguards.
evidence_requirements: State that the ASL-3 action was precautionary/provisional.
Do not imply a confirmed catastrophic capability threshold was crossed.

claims.narration_excerpt: Predictions here diverge sharply.
evidence_requirements: Remove unsupported unemployment figures unless primary sources are added.
Use exposure-versus-outcome distinction.
claims.recommended_rewrite: Forecasts range widely, but the strongest cross-country evidence measures task exposure rather than confirmed job loss.

claims.narration_excerpt: Nobody actually knows yet.
evidence_requirements: Show one-in-four occupational exposure and the ILO warning that exposure is not a job-loss forecast.

claims.narration_excerpt: Models, compute, data, and cloud infrastructure are consolidating into a small number of companies.
evidence_requirements: Add an authoritative competition, cloud-market or compute-concentration source before full render.

claims.narration_excerpt: The AI Act imposes extra obligations on the largest general-purpose models.
evidence_requirements: Show 10^25 FLOP presumption and additional obligations.
State threshold may be updated and is rebuttable/designation-based.

claims.narration_excerpt: Compliance costs may be easier for established companies to absorb.
evidence_requirements: Present as distributional analysis, not as a measured outcome of the AI Act.
Avoid claiming the law has already entrenched incumbents.

claims.narration_excerpt: Neither side has a clean claim to safety.
evidence_requirements: Show benefits and risks on both sides.
Do not present open-weight and closed-access systems as binary or uniform categories.

claims.narration_excerpt: Independent evaluations and audits before systems ship.
evidence_requirements: Show evaluation methods and their limitations.
Do not call an evaluation a safety certification.

claims.narration_excerpt: Mandatory incident reporting and legal accountability.
evidence_requirements: Differentiate existing EU obligations from the film's broader policy recommendations.

claims.narration_excerpt: The greatest danger may be a system that rewards speed and dominance while spreading the risk.
evidence_requirements: Treat as the film's synthesis, visually built from earlier verified evidence rather than a new factual claim.
